mnemonics: CAR: Correlation in Autoregressive Residuals. Regression models often have AR structure in their errors.
keyPoints: Regression models can have serially correlated error terms, often modeled as ARMA processes.
The presence of autocorrelated errors violates OLS assumptions, requiring specialized estimation techniques like GLS.
The Durbin-Watson test is commonly used to detect first-order autocorrelation in regression residuals.
commonMistakes: Applying standard OLS to time series data without checking for autocorrelation in residuals.
Confusing the regression model's error term with the underlying time series process of the dependent variable.
